summary: The main claim is: At the time of decision, Alice and Bob have a shared knowledge of who is first, and also only the second one can make a conditional commitment.
R3: Your final decision is allowed to be conditional on the final decision of some other commitment, if and only if your freeze\ time comes after the freeze\ time of that other commitment.

The main claim is: This means that we need to determine the computation required to simulate the process of evolution from the molecular level to get a true upper bound.
The question then becomes whether that should be our target metric, if our goal is to estimate the amount of computation done by evolution .
